level_signals: 3+ years of product management or engineering experience
Salary range $180,000-$215,000 consistent with mid-senior IC
Own roadmap for a core area, partner directly with founders and leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of product management or engineering experience, ideally building developer tools, infrastructure products, or technical SaaS platforms.
Deeply technical — you can hold your own in conversations about API design, system architecture, data pipelines, and debugging production issues.
Bias toward action — you'd rather ship, learn, and iterate than build the perfect solution upfront.
Strong written and verbal communicator who can align cross-functional teams around a shared vision.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent technical background.
Previous experience at a high-growth startup or developer-focused company.
Hands-on experience building with LLMs, agents, or AI applications.

scope.surface: LangSmith platform — observability, evaluation, and production reliability of AI systems (tracing, annotation, run rules, evaluations)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, developer-facing platform serving startups and enterprises
primary_metrics_inferred: Platform reliability and uptime for production AI systems
Evaluation system scale (10 to 10,000 test cases)
core_responsibilities: Own the roadmap for a core area of the LangSmith platform.
Work with customers to understand their challenges and define new product features.
Work with engineering to define scope, make technical tradeoffs, and ship high-impact features quickly.
Help shape product strategy alongside our founders and leadership team.

green_flags: Series B company with strong VC backing (IVP, Sequoia, Benchmark, CapitalG, Sapphire)
Explicitly encourages shipping and iteration culture
Broad customer base including Fortune 500 signals product maturity
red_flags: Location/remote policy not stated in JD — unclear if role is onsite, hybrid, or remote
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned
No mention of team size or reporting structure
3+ years is a relatively low stated bar; actual bar may be higher given the salary range and scope

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope; not stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from statement about EU/UK/APAC regional compensation)
ambiguities: No explicit location or remote/hybrid policy is stated in the JD body. Cities and work arrangement are unclear.
The 3+ years requirement combines PM and engineering experience as alternatives ('product management or engineering'), making it difficult to classify as strictly PM-years.

'ideally building developer tools, infrastructure products, or technical SaaS platforms' could be interpreted as a must-have context qualifier or merely a preference; treated as contextual qualifier to the years requirement rather than a separate must-have.
